source: REAL headless FCP PAEBevel (UUID 9C655247-…), default light (Light Angle NOT probe-drivable — flat injection leaves output identical across angles).
probe: flat gray input=100, uniform RGBA, Bevel Width=0.1 (band=54px); read the tinted band value ~10px in from each frame edge
band_width_law: band_px = BevelWidth * 0.28125 * maxDim (verified: width0.1 -> cutoff at x=54 exactly, both engine and headless)
engine_bug: engine bevelFilter uses the OLD gradient-normal-dot model (PHASE-2 TODO P2-bevel-1 said to replace) and its inward-normal is INVERTED at the frame border (nx=-gx/gmag points toward transparent), so it DARKENS the band to ~0 instead of lightening. Band GEOMETRY (width/cutoff) is correct; TINT is wrong (9.5 dB, signed -2.8/-5.1/-9.5 growing with width).
fix_needed: port FCP's verbatim OFFSET-ACCUMULATION: re-draw the layer as 4 directionally-offset (offset=halfWidth), |cos(theta+k)|-tinted (k in {0,-45,-90,+45}) copies composited by HgcBevel (out=mix(dest,color,color.a); a=max). The flat additive band toward the light Color matches the measured plateau. Rotation unverifiable (angle not drivable) but the default-light tints above pin the k-to-edge assignment for a regression check.

decoded_mix_law.formula: band pixel = mix(input, T_edge, 0.80) = input*0.20 + T_edge*0.80, clipped [0,255]
decoded_mix_law.verified: reproduces all measured points (input 50/100/150/200 x TOP/BOT/LEFT) within 1 level
decoded_mix_law.note: T_edge = 255 * lightColor * lobe_edge. The four |cos(theta+k)| lobes (k in {0,-45,-90,+45}) don't cleanly fit a single-cosine (resid 0.097), so the exact facet->edge mapping needs the bevelHe vertex-color disasm. Light Angle is NOT probe-drivable so a general angle-driven rewrite is UNVERIFIABLE against headless — only this default-light band is measurable. A future fix should port the verbatim offset-accumulation and CALIBRATE the default to these T targets (regression check).

CRITICAL_mechanism_finding_2026-07-23.finding: FCP Bevel does NOT follow the layer's ALPHA boundary. A bright opaque DISC gets NO bevel (all edges ~100 unchanged). An INSET opaque RECT gets NO bevel on its own alpha edge (inside-top stays 100) — instead the FRAME RECTANGLE border is beveled, and the band extends INTO the transparent frame border (frame edge alpha went 0->204, rgb->255).
CRITICAL_mechanism_finding_2026-07-23.implication: Bevel bevels the FULL-FRAME RECTANGLE edges (the render DOD/canvas rect), not the alpha silhouette. The engine's per-pixel ALPHA-GRADIENT normal model is architecturally wrong on BOTH counts: (1) it follows alpha edges (FCP follows the frame rect), (2) its inward-normal is inverted so it darkens. The band tint law mix(input,T_edge,0.80) with T={TOP255,sides135,bot114} is measured for the frame-rect edges under the default light.
CRITICAL_mechanism_finding_2026-07-23.fix: Rewrite as a frame-rectangle bevel: for each of the 4 canvas edges, lay a band_px-wide band (band_px=Width*0.28125*maxDim) inward, tint = mix(pixel, T_edge, 0.80*opacity) where T_edge=255*lightColor*lobe_edge, and EXTEND the band into the transparent border (alpha rises). Corners blend two edges. Only Stylized__Panels_Across ships it (opaque full-frame layer -> the frame-rect bevel is exactly what shows).

STATUS: FIXED+VERIFIED 2026-07-23: engine bevelFilter rewritten to the frame-rectangle model; spatial.PAEBevel VERIFIED 47.3 dB (was 9.5 DIVERGED). Corner rule = NEAREST edge wins (verified at TL (89,66): dLeft<dTop -> LEFT tint 115 not TOP 212).
